engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Panda Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Panda in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Fiat Panda 169A4.000 engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine causes valve damage if belt snaps after 60k miles.
Injector seal leaks - fuel smell and misfires from degraded injector collars on high-mileage units.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup reduces power and triggers warning lights on Euro 4/5 models.

cards.altText: Fiat Panda 199A2.000 engine - diesel 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon deposits restrict flow causing power loss and limp mode on urban-driven units.
DPF regeneration failure - blocked particulate filter triggers warning light and reduced performance.
Turbo wastegate actuator wear - boost control issues cause P2262 codes and inconsistent power delivery.

cards.altText: Fiat Panda 312A2.000 engine - petrol 0.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Camshaft wear premature - known issue on TwinAir units causing noise and reduced valve timing accuracy.
Oil consumption excessive - PCV valve design leads to increased oil burn on high-mileage examples.
Turbocharger reliability - small turbo units require careful maintenance to avoid premature failure.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,400-£1,800 (supply only) Estimated from comparable TwinAir units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Fiat Panda Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Fiat Panda across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The workhorse 1.2L 8v petrol powering most UK Pandas - simple, affordable, but timing belt critical.
compatibleVariants: Active, Dynamic, Emotion, Pop, Lounge, Trekking, Van (169 & 312 chassis)
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causes valve damage on interference engine - replace at 60k miles
Injector seal leaks cause fuel smell and misfires on high-mileage units
EGR valve clogging reduces power and triggers warning lights on Euro 4/5 models
entries.cta: Get quotes for 169A4.000 Fiat Panda engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Updated Euro 5 version of the 1.2L with revised emissions calibration for post-2012 Pandas.
compatibleVariants: Active, Dynamic, Emotion, Pop, Lounge, Van (312 chassis)
commonFailures: Timing belt failure remains critical - interference design requires preventative replacement
PCV valve wear increases oil consumption on high-mileage examples
Camshaft position sensor faults cause intermittent misfire codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for 312A1.000 Fiat Panda engine replacement

entries.title: 1.1L Petrol
entries.history: The classic 1.1L 8v petrol for early Pandas - simple, reliable, now sought for restoration projects.
compatibleVariants: Active, Dynamic, Classic (169 chassis early production)
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage - replace at 60k miles
External oil leaks from rocker cover gasket on high-mileage units
Cooling system wear leads to overheating under load
entries.cta: Get quotes for 188A4.000 Fiat Panda engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: Fiat's efficient 1.3L MultiJet diesel - popular with high-mileage urban commuters, EGR prone.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging causes power loss and limp mode on short-journey urban driving
DPF regeneration failure triggers warning light and restricts performance
Turbo wastegate actuator wear causes boost control issues and P2262 codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for 199A2.000 Fiat Panda engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 5 updated MultiJet with DPF compatibility for post-2012 Pandas - emissions compliant.
commonFailures: DPF soot accumulation requires active regeneration - blocked filters cause limp mode
EGR cooler carbon buildup reduces efficiency and triggers fault codes
AdBlue system prep required for future emissions compliance in some regions
entries.cta: Get quotes for A13DTE Fiat Panda engine replacement

entries.title: 0.9L Petrol
entries.history: Fiat's innovative 0.9L TwinAir turbo - compact, efficient, but camshaft wear requires specialist attention.
compatibleVariants: Pop, Lounge, 4x4, Cross (312 chassis TwinAir variants)
commonFailures: Camshaft wear premature - known issue requiring upgraded components in specialist rebuilds
Oil consumption excessive due to PCV valve design - monitor levels closely
Turbocharger reliability requires careful maintenance to avoid premature failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for 312A2.000 Fiat Panda engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Fiat Panda engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Panda Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Panda's 1.2L petrol and 1.3L MultiJet diesel dominate UK replacement enquiries, with timing belt oversights and EGR clogging the most common failure triggers. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Panda.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Fiat Panda 1.2L Petrol Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Panda Active, Dynamic, Emotion, Van (2003-2024, 169A4.000 & 312A1.000 1.2L petrol)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if belt not replaced per schedule
problems.rootCause: The 1.2L petrol is an interference engine - if the timing belt snaps, valves collide with pistons causing catastrophic damage requiring full rebuild or replacement.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Belt Replacement - Replace belt, tensioner, water pump before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Belt kit installation with tensioner reset and coolant refresh
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure - recommended at 60k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new timing kit, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores reliability - best value when valve damage has occurred

problems.recommendation: If your Panda's timing belt is due or overdue, invest in preventative replacement immediately - the £280-£450 specialist cost is far cheaper than the £1,100+ rebuild required after belt failure. On a Panda worth £500-£3,500, a rebuilt engine with warranty often makes more financial sense than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.2L petrol engine prices for your Fiat Panda

problems.h4: EGR Valve Clogging - Why Fiat Panda 1.3L MultiJet Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Panda 4x4, Cross, Trekking, Dynamic (2003-2024, 199A2.000 & A13DTE 1.3L diesel)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000-110,000 miles, especially on short-journey urban use
problems.rootCause: Carbon deposits from exhaust recirculation clog the EGR valve and cooler, restricting airflow and triggering limp mode to protect the engine from damage.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Clean/Replace - Clean valve or fit upgraded replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR valve removal, cleaning or replacement with improved design unit
repairOptions.longevity: Restores power temporarily - clogging may recur on high-mileage urban units

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt MultiJet with EGR update
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt diesel with upgraded EGR cooler, DPF check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution - addresses root cause and other wear items simultaneously

problems.recommendation: For Pandas under 80k miles with isolated EGR issues, cleaning or replacing the valve offers good value. However, if your 1.3L MultiJet has over 100k miles or shows multiple warning lights, a rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components often proves more cost-effective than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 1.3L MultiJet rebuilt engines for your Fiat Panda

problems.h4: TwinAir Camshaft Wear - Why Fiat Panda 0.9L Turbo Engines Need Specialist Attention
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Panda TwinAir Pop, Lounge, 4x4 (2012-2024, 312A2.000 0.9L turbo petrol)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 50,000-80,000 miles on units without camshaft upgrade
problems.rootCause: The TwinAir's compact turbo design places high stress on camshaft lobes - premature wear causes noise, reduced valve timing accuracy and eventual loss of performance.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Camshaft Replacement - Fit upgraded camshaft components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Camshaft removal, upgraded lobes installation, timing reset and oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses known weakness - recommended for all TwinAir rebuilds

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Specialist Rebuilt TwinAir - Supply & fit with camshaft upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt TwinAir with mandatory camshaft upgrade, turbo check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value for enthusiast owners wanting to retain TwinAir performance

problems.recommendation: TwinAir owners should only accept rebuilds that include the upgraded camshaft components - this addresses the engine's known weakness. Given the TwinAir's niche appeal and higher rebuild cost, compare specialist quotes carefully before committing.
problems.cta: Compare specialist TwinAir rebuild prices for your Fiat Panda

problems.h4: DPF Regeneration Failure - Why Fiat Panda Diesel Owners Face Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Panda Cross, 4x4, Dynamic Euro 5 (2012-2024, A13DTE 1.3L diesel)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000-120,000 miles, especially on short-journey urban use
problems.rootCause: Diesel particulate filters require active regeneration cycles - frequent short trips prevent complete soot burn-off, leading to blockage, warning lights and restricted performance.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF Clean/Replace - Professional cleaning or replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: DPF removal, ash cleaning or replacement, ECU reset and regeneration cycle
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function temporarily - driving style affects recurrence risk

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit with DPF system check
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt diesel with DPF condition assessment, EGR update, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution - addresses DPF alongside other wear items

problems.recommendation: If your Panda diesel is used primarily for short urban journeys, DPF issues are likely to recur. Consider whether a petrol replacement engine (ULEZ compliant) might offer better long-term value for city driving, or ensure regular motorway runs to support DPF regeneration.
problems.cta: Compare diesel vs petrol replacement engine options for your Fiat Panda

problems.h4: Injector Seal Leaks - Why Fiat Panda Petrol Owners Smell Fuel
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Panda Active, Dynamic, Emotion (2003-2024, 169A4.000 & 312A1.000 1.2L petrol)
problems.rootCause: Injector collar seals degrade with heat and age, allowing fuel vapour to escape - causing smell, potential misfires and increased emissions.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Seal Replacement - Replace injector collars and seals
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Injector removal, new seal installation, ECU coding if required
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective fix for isolated seal issues on otherwise healthy engines

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit with seal refresh
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with all seals refreshed, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when multiple wear items present or engine has high mileage

problems.recommendation: Injector seal replacement is a straightforward, affordable fix if your Panda's engine is otherwise healthy. However, if your 1.2L petrol has over 90k miles or shows other warning signs, investing in a rebuilt engine with all seals refreshed often proves more economical long-term.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 1.2L petrol rebuilt engines with seal refresh for your Fiat Panda

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Fiat Panda
finalCta.paragraph: From timing belt oversights on the 1.2L petrol to EGR clogging on the 1.3L MultiJet, Fiat Panda engine failures are common but manageable. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Fiat Panda specialists - every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Fiat Panda Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Fiat Panda and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Fiat Panda 1.1L Petrol Engines

commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure causes valve damage; external oil leaks from rocker cover on high-mileage units

items.title: Fiat Panda 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L 8v petrol is the Panda's most common engine - simple, affordable and ULEZ compliant, but timing belt maintenance is critical due to interference design.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage; injector seal leaks cause fuel smell; EGR clogging on Euro 4/5 models
items.cta: Compare Fiat Panda 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Panda 0.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 0.9L TwinAir turbo petrol offers spirited performance in a compact package - popular with enthusiasts but requires specialist rebuilds addressing known camshaft wear.
commonFailurePoints: Camshaft wear premature on units without upgrade; oil consumption from PCV valve design; turbo reliability requires careful maintenance
items.cta: Compare Fiat Panda 0.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Panda 1.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.3L MultiJet turbo diesel delivers strong fuel economy for high-mileage commuters - EGR and DPF maintenance critical for urban-driven examples.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging causes power loss; DPF regeneration failure on short-journey use; turbo wastegate actuator wear triggers boost codes
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Panda 1.3L diesel engines

engineYears.h2: Fiat Panda Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Fiat Panda's UK production timeline (2003-2024), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Panda's UK return introduced simple 1.1L and 1.2L petrol plus 1.3L MultiJet diesel - timing belt maintenance critical from day one.
keyChanges: Fiat Panda relaunched on new 169 chassis platform
1.3L MultiJet diesel (199A2.000) added for high-mileage buyers
Active, Dynamic and Emotion trim levels launched
knownFor: Simple, affordable city car with low running costs
Timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage - replace at 60k miles
Early 1.3L MultiJet EGR valves prone to clogging on urban use
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2006 Fiat Panda replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Panda 1.2 timing belt replacement • Fiat Panda 1.3 EGR valve clogging • Fiat Panda used engine low mileage
enquiries: Fiat Panda used engine low mileage

years.description: Euro 5 emissions updates refined the 1.2L petrol and 1.3L MultiJet - EGR calibration improvements reduced clogging on later units.
keyChanges: Euro 5 emissions compliance introduced for 1.2L petrol (312A1.000 code)
1.3L MultiJet received EGR calibration updates reducing clogging frequency
Trekking and Van variants added to range
knownFor: Improved EGR calibration on late 169 diesels reduced urban clogging issues
Timing belt remains critical - interference design unchanged
Strong residual values for 4x4 and Trekking enthusiast variants
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2012 Fiat Panda replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Panda 1.2 petrol rebuilt engine • Fiat Panda 1.3 MultiJet EGR fix • Fiat Panda 4x4 engine replacement

years.description: The all-new 312 chassis brought updated styling, the innovative TwinAir turbo petrol, and Euro 5 diesel compliance as standard.
keyChanges: New 312 chassis platform with improved safety and refinement
0.9L TwinAir turbo petrol (312A2.000) introduced - 85hp from compact turbo
1.2L petrol updated to 312A1.000 Euro 5 code with revised emissions
1.3L MultiJet diesel now A13DTE Euro 5 with DPF as standard
mainEngines: A13DTE - 1.3L diesel Euro 5 with DPF (Cross, 4x4, Dynamic)
knownFor: TwinAir's innovative turbo design delivers strong performance but camshaft wear requires specialist attention
DPF regeneration critical on Euro 5 diesels - short journeys increase blockage risk
Cross variant gains rugged styling and enhanced off-road capability
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2016 Fiat Panda replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Panda TwinAir camshaft upgrade • Fiat Panda Cross diesel replacement • Fiat Panda DPF regeneration fix

years.description: Mid-cycle updates refined emissions systems and component reliability - confirm exact engine code before ordering replacements.
keyChanges: Emissions system refinements for stricter Euro 6d-TEMP testing
Updated ECU calibration across all engine codes improving drivability
Cross and 4x4 variants gain enhanced trim and equipment levels
TwinAir receives PCV valve update reducing oil consumption
mainEngines: 312A2.000 - 0.9L TwinAir turbo with PCV update (Pop, Lounge, 4x4)
A13DTE - 1.3L diesel Euro 6 with improved DPF strategy (Cross, 4x4)
knownFor: Improved PCV design on TwinAir reduces oil consumption on high-mileage units
DPF regeneration strategy refined on late 312 diesels for urban compatibility
Strong enthusiast following for 4x4 and Cross variants with low-mileage examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2020 Fiat Panda replacement engines

years.description: Final production years offer lowest mileage replacement engines - ideal for preserving late-model Pandas with minimal wear.
keyChanges: Final production run of 312 chassis Panda before model discontinuation
Lowest mileage used engines available from late registrations
Continued parts support and specialist rebuild availability confirmed
knownFor: Lowest mileage replacement engines available for late-model Pandas
Strong residual values for well-maintained Cross and 4x4 enthusiast variants
Petrol variants remain ULEZ compliant avoiding London clean air charges
years.cta: Get quotes for 2021-2024 Fiat Panda replacement engines

faq.h2: Fiat Panda Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Fiat Panda engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Fiat Panda engine replacement costs range from £400-£900 for verified low-mileage used units to £1,100-£1,800 for rebuilt engines, depending on engine code, variant and condition. Get tailored quotes by entering your registration.
keyPoints: Used engines: £400-£900 supply only, mileage verified
Rebuilt petrol 1.2L: £1,100-£1,600 supply only
Rebuilt diesel 1.3L: £1,150-£1,600 supply only
TwinAir specialist rebuilds: £1,400-£1,800 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £350-£650 typically
items.cta: Compare Fiat Panda engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine code does my Fiat Panda have?
items.answer: Check your engine bay for a metal plate showing codes like 169A4.000 (1.2L petrol), 199A2.000 (1.3L diesel) or 312A2.000 (0.9L TwinAir), or use our free reg lookup to identify your exact unit instantly.
keyPoints: 1.2L petrol codes: 169A4.000 (Euro 4), 312A1.000 (Euro 5)
1.3L diesel codes: 199A2.000 (Euro 4), A13DTE (Euro 5)
0.9L TwinAir code: 312A2.000 only
Code location: engine bay plate or VIN decoder
Enter reg above for instant code identification
items.cta: Get your Fiat Panda engine code identified free

items.question: Is the Fiat Panda 1.2 petrol engine reliable?
items.answer: The 1.2L petrol is generally reliable with proper maintenance, but timing belt replacement at 60k miles is critical - failure causes catastrophic valve damage on this interference engine.
keyPoints: Simple 8v design with proven long-term durability
Timing belt must be replaced every 60k miles or 5 years
Injector seal leaks common on high-mileage units
Rebuilt units with upgraded components offer best value
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.2L petrol engine prices for your Panda

items.question: Can I fit a Fiat Panda engine myself?
items.answer: Supply-only engines are available for DIY/garage fitting, but specialist installation is recommended to ensure ECU coding, warranty validity and post-install diagnostics are handled correctly.
keyPoints: Supply-only pricing saves £350-£650 vs supply & fit
ECU coding required on many Panda variants post-install
Specialist fitting preserves 12-month warranty coverage
Post-install diagnostics and road test recommended
items.cta: Find Fiat Panda engine supply & fit deals UK

items.question: What warranty comes with a Fiat Panda replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty covering internal components, with terms confirmed by your chosen UK specialist supplier.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
6-month warranty typical on verified used engines
Warranty covers internal components and labour at supplying garage
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier pre-purchase
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Panda engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: How long does Fiat Panda engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical timeline is 3-7 days from quote to road-ready: 1-3 days for engine sourcing/delivery plus 1 day for specialist fitting and post-install diagnostics at your chosen UK garage.
keyPoints: Engine sourcing: 1-3 days depending on code availability
Delivery: Next-day mainland UK dispatch on most orders
Fitting: 1 day at specialist garage including diagnostics
Total: 3-7 days typical from quote to back on road
items.cta: Book Fiat Panda engine supply & fit appointment today

items.question: Will a replacement Fiat Panda engine pass MoT?
items.answer: Yes - all engines supplied through EngineMarket are rebuilt or verified to meet UK MoT emissions standards, with documentation provided to support your MoT test after installation.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines tested to meet original emissions specifications
Documentation provided for MoT examiner verification
Petrol Pandas (1.1L/1.2L) are ULEZ compliant Euro 4/5
Diesel Pandas require Euro 5 confirmation for ULEZ exemption
items.cta: Check Fiat Panda engine MoT compliance before ordering

items.question: Is a Fiat Panda engine ULEZ compliant London?
items.answer: Petrol Fiat Pandas (1.1L/1.2L) meet Euro 4/5 standards and are ULEZ compliant, avoiding London's £12.50/day charge - diesel variants require Euro 5 confirmation for exemption.
keyPoints: 1.1L/1.2L petrol Pandas: Euro 4/5 compliant, ULEZ exempt
1.3L diesel Pandas: Verify Euro 5 (A13DTE code) for ULEZ exemption
Euro 4 diesel (199A2.000) may incur daily ULEZ charges
Enter reg above to confirm your Panda's ULEZ status instantly
items.cta: Compare ULEZ compliant Fiat Panda engine options

items.question: Can I upgrade my Fiat Panda from 1.2L petrol to 1.3L diesel?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but require extensive modification to fuel system, ECU, exhaust and wiring - for most owners, replacing with the original-spec engine offers better value and reliability.
keyPoints: Diesel swap requires fuel tank, lines, ECU and exhaust changes
Wiring loom and dashboard instrumentation may need updating
MoT and insurance implications require specialist advice
Original-spec replacement typically offers better cost/benefit
items.cta: Speak to a Fiat Panda engine specialist about your options

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt, remanufactured and used Fiat Panda engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines replace critical wear items with upgrades targeting Panda failure points; remanufactured units are factory-spec with all new components; used engines are low-mileage verified budget options with shorter warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: Critical components replaced, upgraded for known failures
Remanufactured: Factory-spec rebuild with all new components
Used: Low-mileage verified, budget option with 6-month warranty
Best value depends on your Panda's age, mileage and budget
